key_insight: FEVessel enables mesh-independent 3D pressure-vessel analysis by pretrained, label-free finite element learning, achieving accurate predictions even on degenerate meshes.
engineering_relevance: It reduces repeated solve cost and avoids manual mesh repair, making industrial pressure-vessel simulation faster and more robust across changing materials, geometries, and loads.
method: Encodes each vessel as a point cloud with coordinate, material, and load channels.
Pretrains a Transolver on total potential energy instead of FEM-generated labels.
Uses the prediction as a warm start for iterative algebraic multigrid solving.
evidence_signal: The abstract reports 1.35% relative displacement error, 2.07% strain error, a 9.2× wall-clock speedup, and sub-3.66% error on inverted/sliver meshes.
watch_item: Performance still depends on the quality of the pretrained operator and careful integration with the downstream solver, especially when transferring to very sparse point densities or new vessel regimes.
paper_title: FEVessel: Mesh-Independent Analysis of 3D Pressure Vessels with the Label-Free Pretrained Finite Element Method
